mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 10 percent signals severe economic distress that triggers immediate equity sell-offs and negative forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 10 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings expectations higher, yielding positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 20 days with a p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below 90 percent reflects a supply-side shock that suppresses corporate output and generates negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where INDPRO stays below 90, invalidating the supply contraction penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product signals strengthening economic activity that immediately boosts market sentiment and produces positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, liquidity conditions become exceptionally loose, compressing risk premiums and forcing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent, with a mean return below zero over 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and corporate profitability, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns in more than half of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL index levels above 260 signal entrenched inflation that forces immediate equity deleveraging and negative forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 65% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, contradicting the inflation penalty mechanism.
